devanagari: श्रीकृष्णस्याह्निककृत्यवर्णनं युधिष्ठिरसंदेशमादाय नारदस्य । जरासंधकारानिबद्धनृपाणां संदेशमादाय दूतस्य चागमनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । अथोषस्यु...
english_devnagari: śrīkṛṣṇasyāhnikakṛtyavarṇanaṃ yudhiṣṭhirasaṃdeśamādāya nāradasya jarāsaṃdhakārānibaddhanṛpāṇāṃ saṃdeśamādāya dūtasya cāgamanam śrīśuka uvāca athoṣasyupavṛttāyāṃ kukkuṭān kūjato gṛhītakaṇṭhyaḥ patibhiḥ bhādhavyo virahāturāḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Now, at the approach of the dawning day, the queens of Māḍhava, each of whom was sleeping with the arms of their Lord round their necks, cursed the cocks crowing at dawn, as they were nervously anxious about their (prospective) separation from him.

devanagari: वयांस्यरूरुवन् कृष्णं बोधयन्तीव वन्दिनः । गायत्स्वलिष्वनिद्राणि मन्दारवनवायुभिः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vayāṃsyarūruvan kṛṣṇaṃ bodhayantīva vandinaḥ gāyatsvaliṣvanidrāṇi mandāravanavāyubhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ budh ], [nominative plural from √ budh ], [vocative dual from √ budh ], [vocative plural from √ budh ], [accusative dual from √ budh ], [accusative plural from √ budh ]
[nominative single from √ budh ], [vocative single from √ budh ]

translation: Aroused from sleep by the breezes wafting through Mandāra groves while the black bees were humming sweetly, birds began to sing and warble like bards to wake up Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: मुहूर्तं तं तु वैदर्भी नामृष्यद् अतिशोभनम् । परिरम्भणविश्लेषात् प्रियबाह्वन्तरं गता ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: muhūrtaṃ taṃ tu vaidarbhī nāmṛṣyad atiśobhanam parirambhaṇaviśleṣāt priyabāhvantaraṃ gatā
translation: Closely hugged within the arms of her lover, even Rukmiṇī would not tolerate that extremely auspicious period (called Brāhma muhūrta) for fear of separation from his embrace.

devanagari: ब्राह्मे मुहूर्त उत्थाय वार्युपस्पृश्य माधवः । दध्यौ प्रसन्नकरण आत्मानं तमसः परम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhme muhūrta utthāya vāryupaspṛśya mādhavaḥ dadhyau prasannakaraṇa ātmānaṃ tamasaḥ param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Getting up in the Brāhma period (two hours before the sun-rise), Śrī Kṛṣṇa would wash his hands and feet and rinse his mouth. Sipping three spoonfuls of water as ācamana, he with a serene mind, used to meditate on the Self (ātman) who is beyond the range of darkness (i.e. Prakṛti).

devanagari: एकं स्वयंज्योतिरनन्यमव्ययं । स्वसंस्थया नित्यनिरस्तकल्मषम् । ब्रह्माख्यमस्योद्भवनाशहेतुभिः । स्वशक्तिभिर्लक्षितभावनिर्वृतिम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaṃ svayaṃjyotirananyamavyayaṃ svasaṃsthayā nityanirastakalmaṣam brahmākhyamasyodbhavanāśahetubhiḥ svaśaktibhirlakṣitabhāvanirvṛtim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ lakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ lakṣ class 10 verb]
[vocative single from √ lakṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ lakṣ class 10 verb]

translation: He used to contemplate upon Ātman also designated as Brahman, Who is one without any limiting condition (upādhi), self-luminous, immutable; Who has been eternally free from the taint of Avidyā (Nescience) by virtue of His being established in His own self; Whose existence and blissfulness of nature are revealed by His own potencies that bring about the creation, maintenance and destruction of the universe.

devanagari: अथाप्लुतोऽम्भस्यमले यथाविधि । क्रियाकलापं परिधाय वाससी । चकार सन्ध्योपगमादि सत्तमो । हुतानलो ब्रह्म जजाप वाग्यतः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpluto kriyākalāpaṃ paridhāya vāsasī cakāra sandhyopagamādi sattamo hutānalo brahma jajāpa vāgyataḥ

translation: After meditation, he took bath in clean water after Śāstric injunctions. Putting on two garments, he performed the Twilight-prayer (Sandhya), and other (prescribed observances. The prominent one among the righteous persons then offered oblations to sacrificial fire and silently repeated the Gāyatrī mantra.

devanagari: उपस्थायार्कमुद्यन्तं तर्पयित्वात्मनः कलाः । देवान् ऋषीन् पितॄन् वृद्धान् विप्रानभ्यर्च्य चात्मवान् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: upasthāyārkamudyantaṃ tarpayitvātmanaḥ kalāḥ devān ṛṣīn pitṝn vṛddhān viprānabhyarcya cātmavān
translation: He used to offer prayers to the rising sun and pour out water libations to gods, sages, his ancestors (pitṛs)—all of whom were his own rays—and with a controlled mind, he used to worship the elderly persons and Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: धेनूनां रुक्मश्रृङ्गीणां साध्वीनां मौक्तिकस्रजाम् । पयस्विनीनां गृष्टीनां सवत्सानां सुवाससाम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: dhenūnāṃ rukmaśrṛṅgīṇāṃ sādhvīnāṃ mauktikasrajām payasvinīnāṃ gṛṣṭīnāṃ savatsānāṃ suvāsasām
translation: To Brāhmaṇas who were adorned with ornaments, along with silken cloth, deer-hide (Kṛṣṇājina) and sesamum seeds, he used to give every day thirteen thousand and eighty- four cows, all of them docile, with horns capped with gold tops and hoofs plated in silver and necks adorned with pearl-necklaces, yielding abundant milk, with only one freshly yeaned calf, accompanied by their calves, beautifully caparisoned.

devanagari: ददौ रूप्यखुराग्राणां क्षौमाजिनतिलैः सह । अलङ्कृतेभ्यो विप्रेभ्यो बद्वं बद्वं दिने दिने ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: dadau rūpyakhurāgrāṇāṃ kṣaumājinatilaiḥ saha alaṅkṛtebhyo viprebhyo badvaṃ badvaṃ dine dine

translation: To Brāhmaṇas who were adorned with ornaments, along with silken cloth, deer-hide (Kṛṣṇājina) and sesamum seeds, he used to give every day thirteen thousand and eighty- four cows, all of them docile, with horns capped with gold tops and hoofs plated in silver and necks adorned with pearl-necklaces, yielding abundant milk, with only one freshly yeaned calf, accompanied by their calves, beautifully caparisoned.

devanagari: गोविप्रदेवतावृद्ध गुरून् भूतानि सर्वशः । नमस्कृत्यात्मसंभूतीः मङ्गलानि समस्पृशत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: govipradevatāvṛddha gurūn bhūtāni sarvaśaḥ namaskṛtyātmasaṃbhūtīḥ maṅgalāni samaspṛśat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: He used to pay respects to cows, Brāhmaṇas, elderly persons, preceptors and all beings who were his own manifestations, and he used to touch auspicious things (like a towny coloured cow, etc.).

devanagari: आत्मानं भूषयामास नरलोकविभूषणम् । वासोभिर्भूषणैः स्वीयैः दिव्यस्रग् अनुलेपनैः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānaṃ bhūṣayāmāsa naralokavibhūṣaṇam vāsobhirbhūṣaṇaiḥ svīyaiḥ divyasrag anulepanaiḥ
translation: He himself was one of the ornaments of the world. He used to adorn himself with his special garments (e.g. yellow silken loin cloth or pītāmbara) and adornments (e.g. the Kaustubha gem) and his special excellent garlands (e.g. the Vanamālā) and pigments.

devanagari: अवेक्ष्याज्यं तथाऽदर्शं गोवृषद्विजदेवताः । कामांश्च सर्ववर्णानां पौरान्तःपुरचारिणाम् । प्रदाप्य प्रकृतीः कामैः प्रतोष्य प्रत्यनन्दत ॥ ...
english_devnagari: avekṣyājyaṃ tathā kāmāṃśca sarvavarṇānāṃ paurāntaḥpuracāriṇām pradāpya prakṛtīḥ kāmaiḥ pratoṣya pratyanandata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]

translation: Seeing the reflection of his face in liquid ghee and a mirror and cast his (first) glances at cows, bulls, Brāhmaṇas and gods, he used to take delight in granting the boons and desired objects to people belonging to all classes inhabiting in his city or dwelling in his harem (alike), and satisfying their requirements by supplying the needs of his counsellors and courtiers.

devanagari: संविभज्याग्रतो विप्रान् स्रक्ताम्बूलानुलेपनैः । सुहृदः प्रकृतीर्दारान् उपायुङ्क्त ततः स्वयम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃvibhajyāgrato viprān sraktāmbūlānulepanaiḥ suhṛdaḥ prakṛtīrdārān upāyuṅkta tataḥ svayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: He would first distribute garlands, betel-leaves and sandal-pastes to Brāhmaṇas; then he would honour his friends, ministers and queens with them, and lastly he would accept them for himself.

devanagari: तावत् सूत उपानीय स्यन्दनं परमाद्भुतम् । सुग्रीवाद्यैर्हयैर्युक्तं प्रणम्यावस्थितोऽग्रतः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvat sūta upānīya syandanaṃ paramādbhutam sugrīvādyairhayairyuktaṃ praṇamyāvasthito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: By that time, the charioteer used to bring his most wonderful chariot drawn by Sugrīva and other horses and bowing down to him, he used to stand before him.

devanagari: गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणी सारथेस्तमथारुहत् । सात्यक्युद्धवसंयुक्तः पूर्वाद्रिमिव भास्करः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇī sārathestamathāruhat sātyakyuddhavasaṃyuktaḥ pūrvādrimiva bhāskaraḥ
translation: Thereupon, holding the charioteer’s hand by his own, he used to mount his chariot along with Sātyaki and Uddhava, like unto the sun ascending the eastern mountain.

devanagari: ईक्षितोऽन्तःपुरस्त्रीणां सव्रीडप्रेमवीक्षितैः । कृच्छ्राद् विसृष्टो निरगात् जातहासो हरन् मनः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: īkṣito kṛcchrād visṛṣṭo niragāt jātahāso haran manaḥ

translation: Gazed at by the ladies in the harem with their bashful and affectionate looks and permitted unwillingly by them, he would proceed captivating their hearts with his bewitching smiles.

devanagari: सुधर्माख्यां सभां सर्वैः वृष्णिभिः परिवारितः । प्राविशद् यन्निविष्टानां न सन्त्यङ्ग षडूर्मयः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sudharmākhyāṃ sabhāṃ sarvaiḥ vṛṣṇibhiḥ parivāritaḥ prāviśad yanniviṣṭānāṃ na santyaṅga ṣaḍūrmayaḥ

translation: O king Parīkṣit then surrounded by all Vṛṣṇis, he would enter the assembly-hall called Sudharmā the occupants—members—of which are not subject to six ‘waves’ i.e. changes in human life (viz. hunger, thirst, grief, delusion, old age and death).

devanagari: तत्रोपविष्टः परमासने विभुः । बभौ स्वभासा ककुभोऽवभासयन् । वृतो नृसिंहैर्यदुभिर्यदूत्तमो । यथोडुराजो दिवि तारकागणैः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tatropaviṣṭaḥ paramāsane vibhuḥ babhau svabhāsā kakubho vṛto nṛsiṃhairyadubhiryadūttamo yathoḍurājo divi tārakāgaṇaiḥ

translation: Seated there on an excellent, exalted throne, the Almighty Lord shone illuminating all the cardinal points by his personal lustre. Surrounded by lion-like prominent Yadus, Kṛṣṇa, the best of Yadus, appeared like the moon in the midst of the hosts of constellations and stars.

devanagari: तत्रोपमंत्रिणो राजन् नानाहास्यरसैर्विभुम् । उपतस्थुर्नटाचार्या नर्तक्यस्ताण्डवैः पृथक् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatropamaṃtriṇo rājan nānāhāsyarasairvibhum upatasthurnaṭācāryā nartakyastāṇḍavaiḥ pṛthak

translation: O King! There, the court-jesters waited upon the Lord by providing amusements of various kinds. So did the masters in dancing with their manly dances as well as dancing girls with their special performances severally.

devanagari: मृदङ्गवीणामुरज वेणुतालदरस्वनैः । ननृतुर्जगुस्तुष्टुवुश्च सूतमागधवन्दिनः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: mṛdaṅgavīṇāmuraja veṇutāladarasvanaiḥ nanṛturjagustuṣṭuvuśca sūtamāgadhavandinaḥ

translation: Bards—sūtas, Māgadhas and Bandins danced, sang and extolled him in accompaniment to tabors, lutes, tambourines, flutes, cymbals and conchs.

devanagari: तत्राहुर्ब्राह्मणाः केचित् आसीना ब्रह्मवादिनः । पूर्वेषां पुण्ययशसां राज्ञां चाकथयन् कथाः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāhurbrāhmaṇāḥ kecit āsīnā brahmavādinaḥ pūrveṣāṃ puṇyayaśasāṃ rājñāṃ cākathayan kathāḥ
translation: There were seated some Brāhmaṇas well-versed in the exposition of the Vedas. They elucidated Vedic verses as well as told the stories of ancient kings of sanctifying glory.

devanagari: तत्रैकः पुरुषो राजन् आगतोऽपूर्वदर्शनः । विज्ञापितो भगवते प्रतीहारैः प्रवेशितः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tatraikaḥ puruṣo rājan āgato vijñāpito bhagavate pratīhāraiḥ praveśitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: There arrived a stranger not ever seen before. With the permission of the Lord, he was ushered in his presence by the guards at the door.

devanagari: स नमस्कृत्य कृष्णाय परेशाय कृताञ्जलिः । राज्ञामावेदयद् दुखं जरासन्धनिरोधजम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa namaskṛtya kṛṣṇāya pareśāya kṛtāñjaliḥ rājñāmāvedayad dukhaṃ jarāsandhanirodhajam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Having bowed to the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, he, with his palms folded, submitted to him the miserable plight of kings who were kept in captivity by Jarāsandha.

devanagari: ये च दिग्विजये तस्य सन्नतिं न ययुर्नृपाः । प्रसह्य रुद्धास्तेनासन् अयुते द्वे गिरिव्रजे ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ye ca digvijaye tasya sannatiṃ na yayurnṛpāḥ prasahya ruddhāstenāsan ayute dve girivraje

[nominative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ rudh class 7 verb]

translation:
Those twenty thousand kings who did not submissively acknowledged Jarāsandha’s sovereignty during his conquest of all the quarters (of the world), had been taken captive per force and are incarcerated at Girivraja (mod. Rajgir in Bihar).
(The message of the detained kings:)

devanagari: राजान ऊचुः । कृष्ण कृष्णाप्रमेयात्मन् प्रपन्नभयभञ्जन । वयं त्वां शरणं यामो भवभीताः पृथग्धियः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: rājāna ūcuḥ kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇāprameyātman prapannabhayabhañjana vayaṃ tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ yāmo bhavabhītāḥ pṛthagdhiyaḥ
translation: “O Kṛṣṇa who attract the minds of devotees and of incomprehensible nature. You are the destroyer of the fear of those who seek resort in you. We are of poor intellect and are mortally afraid of Saṃsāra. We seek asylum in you.

devanagari: लोको विकर्मनिरतः कुशले प्रमत्तः । कर्मण्ययं त्वदुदिते भवदर्चने स्वे । यस्तावदस्य बलवानिह जीविताशां । सद्यश्छिनत्त्यनिमिषाय नमोऽस्तु तस...
english_devnagari: loko vikarmanirataḥ kuśale pramattaḥ karmaṇyayaṃ tvadudite bhavadarcane sve yastāvadasya balavāniha jīvitāśāṃ sadyaśchinattyanimiṣāya namo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ yas class 4 verb]

translation: We bow to that ever-vigilant Time-Spirit viz. you who are all powerful and who suddenly cut short the desire for long life of this world while it is deeply involved in doing prohibited acts and is careless and negligent in performing auspicious actions such as your worship and devotion as enjoined by you.

devanagari: लोके भवाञ्जगदिनः कलयावतीर्णः । सद् रक्षणाय खलनिग्रहणाय चान्यः । कश्चित् त्वदीयमतियाति निदेशमीश । किं वा जनः स्वकृतमृच्छति तन्न विद्मः ...
english_devnagari: loke bhavāñjagadinaḥ kalayāvatīrṇaḥ sad rakṣaṇāya khalanigrahaṇāya cānyaḥ kaścit tvadīyamatiyāti nideśamīśa kiṃ vā janaḥ svakṛtamṛcchati tanna vidmaḥ
translation: O Lord, You are the controller of the Universe. You have incarnated in this world by a ray of yours (i.e. Balarāma) for the protection of the righteous and chastisement and destruction of the wicked. We do not understand how somebody else (like Jarāsandha) transgresses your orders (with impunity) and why persons (though protected by you) still take to the path of their Karmas (and experience misery).

devanagari: स्वप्नायितं नृपसुखं परतंत्रमीश । शश्वद्भयेन मृतकेन धुरं वहामः । हित्वा तदात्मनि सुखं त्वदनीहलभ्यं । क्लिश्यामहेऽतिकृपणास्तव माययेह ॥ २...
english_devnagari: svapnāyitaṃ nṛpasukhaṃ parataṃtramīśa śaśvadbhayena mṛtakena dhuraṃ vahāmaḥ hitvā tadātmani sukhaṃ tvadanīhalabhyaṃ kliśyāmahe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ labh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ labh class 1 verb]

translation: The happiness of Kingship contingent as it is on so many conditions, is an empty dream. We carry on the burden of saṃsāra (and the anxiety and responsibility of wife, children, etc.), with our body which is as good as dead and stands constantly in fear of death. We are extremely miserable inasmuch as we abandon the spiritual bliss which is obtainable from you by those who renounce all desires and undergo miseries due to (the inexorable force of) your Māyā.

devanagari: तन्नो भवान् प्रणतशोकहराङ्घ्रियुग्मो । बद्धान् वियुङ्क्ष्व मगधाह्वयकर्मपाशात् । यो भूभुजोऽयुतमतङ्गजवीर्यमेको । बिभ्रद् रुरोध भवने मृगरा...
english_devnagari: tanno bhavān praṇataśokaharāṅghriyugmo baddhān viyuṅkṣva magadhāhvayakarmapāśāt yo bhūbhujo bibhrad rurodha bhavane mṛgarāḍivāvīḥ

translation: The pair of feet of your Lordship destroy the miseries of those who surrender to them humbly. We are at present imprisoned. Be pleased to set us free from the bondage of Karma now called Jarāsandha, the king of Magadhas. Possessed as he is of the strength of ten thousand elephants, he singlehandedly took us, kings, captive and imprisoned us in his palace as a king of beasts (a lion) would do to sheep.

devanagari: यो वै त्वया द्विनवकृत्व उदात्तचक्र । भग्नो मृधे खलु भवन्तमनन्तवीर्यम् । जित्वा नृलोकनिरतं सकृदूढदर्पो । युष्मत्प्रजा रुजति नोऽजित तद्व...
english_devnagari: yo vai tvayā dvinavakṛtva udāttacakra bhagno mṛdhe khalu bhavantamanantavīryam jitvā nṛlokanirataṃ sakṛdūḍhadarpo yuṣmatprajā rujati no

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: O Lord with uplifted discus (Sudarśana), Jarāsandha who was defeated by you eighteen times in the battle, has grown insolent by somehow defeating you in one of them. Though you possess infinite prowess you behave like a human being. But Jarāsandha is grinding us, knowing us to be your subjects. O unconquerable Lord, May you be pleased to do whatever you think proper to do (under these circumstances).

devanagari: दूत उवाच । इति मागधसंरुद्धा भवद्दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः । प्रपन्नाः पादमूलं ते दीनानां शं विधीयताम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: dūta uvāca iti māgadhasaṃruddhā bhavaddarśanakāṅkṣiṇaḥ prapannāḥ pādamūlaṃ te dīnānāṃ śaṃ vidhīyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: (The messenger concludes:) Thus the kings who are imprisoned by Jarāsandha are desirous of seeing your Lordship. They have submitted themselves to the soles of Your feet. Be pleased to bestow happiness on those hapless kings.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । राजदूते ब्रुवत्येवं देवर्षिः परमद्युतिः । बिभ्रत् पिङ्गजटाभारं प्रादुरासीद् यथा रविः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca rājadūte bruvatyevaṃ devarṣiḥ paramadyutiḥ bibhrat piṅgajaṭābhāraṃ prādurāsīd yathā raviḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
While the messenger of the kings was thus delivering the message of the kings, there manifested like the sun, the divine sage Nārada of supreme lustre, wearing gold-coloured matted locks of hair.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text:]
32(A). Nārada entered singing of the exploits and incarnations of his beloved Kṛṣṇa making Kṛṣṇa, his followers and the world as if melt (with the sweet music) and filling them with great delight.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text:]
32(B). (Nārada came in) singing in sweet voice and expressions the delightful acts and incarnation of Lord Kṛṣṇa liquidating heaps of sins and filling the world with extreme joy.

devanagari: तं दृष्ट्वा भगवान् कृष्णः सर्वलोकेश्वरेश्वरः । ववन्द उत्थितः शीर्ष्णा ससभ्यः सानुगो मुदा ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ dṛṣṭvā bhagavān kṛṣṇaḥ sarvalokeśvareśvaraḥ vavanda utthitaḥ śīrṣṇā sasabhyaḥ sānugo mudā
translation: Seeing him, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Ruler of the rulers of the worlds, rose from his seat and bowed him with his head with great delight, along with the members of the assembly and his followers.

devanagari: सभाजयित्वा विधिवत् कृतासनपरिग्रहम् । बभाषे सुनृतैर्वाक्यैः श्रद्धया तर्पयन् मुनिम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sabhājayitvā vidhivat kṛtāsanaparigraham babhāṣe sunṛtairvākyaiḥ śraddhayā tarpayan munim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Having worshipped him with due formalities and seating him comfortably, he addressed him in sweet words, pleasing the sage with his devotion and sincerity.

devanagari: अपि स्विदद्य लोकानां त्रयाणां अकुतोभयम् । ननु भूयान् भगवतो लोकान् पर्यटतो गुणः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: api svidadya lokānāṃ trayāṇāṃ akutobhayam nanu bhūyān bhagavato lokān paryaṭato guṇaḥ
translation: He enquired, “Are all the three worlds now free from fear from all quarters? It is certainly a great advantage to us that your worship ranges over all the worlds (as we are thereby in a position to know what goes on in the world).

devanagari: न हि तेऽविदितं किञ्चित् लोकेषु ईश्वर कर्तृषु । अथ पृच्छामहे युष्मान् पाण्डवानां चिकीर्षितम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: na hi te atha pṛcchāmahe yuṣmān pāṇḍavānāṃ cikīrṣitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ ], [accusative single from √ kṛ ]

translation: To be sure, nothing is unknown to you in all the creation of the Lord. We, therefore, enquire of you what Pāṇḍavas now intend to do.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । दृष्टा माया ते बहुशो दुरत्यया । माया विभो विश्वसृजश्च मायिनः । भूतेषु भूमंश्चरतः स्वशक्तिभिः । वह्नेरिवच्छन्नरुचो न मे...
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvāca dṛṣṭā māyā te bahuśo duratyayā māyā vibho viśvasṛjaśca māyinaḥ bhūteṣu bhūmaṃścarataḥ svaśaktibhiḥ vahnerivacchannaruco na me

translation:
Nārada replied:
O All-pervading almighty Lord, I have witnessed to a great extent a variety of your deluding potencies (māyās)[1] which are, inscrutable and insuperable, and which delude even god Brahmā, the great conjuror, the creator of the universe. O Perfect one I By your powers you abide in all created beings and yet you remain with your splendour undisclosed like fire remaining latent in the logs of wood. (Although you are omniscient, you assume ignorance like a human being). Hence your query causes me no surprise.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
VT: Māyās i.e. the creations or products of these māyās viz. a number of Brahmāṇḍas with all various gods such as Brahmās, Viṣṇus who possess their own māyās which are nothing but the powers imparted to them by you.—I have witnessed these etc.

devanagari: तवेहितं कोऽर्हति साधु वेदितुं । स्वमाययेदं सृजतो नियच्छतः । यद् विद्यमानात् मतयावभासते । तस्मै नमस्ते स्वविलक्षणात्मने ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tavehitaṃ ko svamāyayedaṃ sṛjato niyacchataḥ yad vidyamānāt matayāvabhāsate tasmai namaste svavilakṣaṇātmane

translation: Who is capable of knowing comprehensively what you intend to do, for you create as well as destroy this universe by your Māyā Potency. The universe appears to exist due to your Māyā (though it is unreal). I bow to you who are in essence distinct from everything else and are thus of incomprehensible nature.

devanagari: जीवस्य यः संसरतो विमोक्षणं । न जानतोऽनर्थवहाच्छरीरतः । लीलावतारैः स्वयशः प्रदीपकं । प्राज्वालयत्त्वा तमहं प्रपद्ये ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: jīvasya yaḥ saṃsarato vimokṣaṇaṃ na jānato līlāvatāraiḥ svayaśaḥ pradīpakaṃ prājvālayattvā tamahaṃ prapadye

translation: O Lord! I seek asylum in you who have enkindled the brilliant lamp (beacon-light) of your glory by sportively assuming various incarnations. This light is for those jīvas who wander in the cycle of saṃsāra and do not know the way to Final Emancipation from the bondage of the body which is the source of all evil.

devanagari: अथाप्याश्रावये ब्रह्म नरलोकविडम्बनम् । राज्ञः पैतृष्वसेयस्य भक्तस्य च चिकीर्षितम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: athāpyāśrāvaye brahma naralokaviḍambanam rājñaḥ paitṛṣvaseyasya bhaktasya ca cikīrṣitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ ], [accusative single from √ kṛ ]

translation: Though yourself the Supreme Brahman, you imitate the ways of human beings in the world. I, however, report to you what king Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of your-paternal aunt and your devotee plans to do.

devanagari: यक्ष्यति त्वां मखेन्द्रेण राजसूयेन पाण्डवः । पारमेष्ठ्यकामो नृपतिः तद्भवाननुमोदताम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: yakṣyati tvāṃ makhendreṇa rājasūyena pāṇḍavaḥ pārameṣṭhyakāmo nṛpatiḥ tadbhavānanumodatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu, aspires to be a sovereign ruler of the world. He, therefore, wishes to worship you with Rājasūya, the greatest of all sacrifices. You may therefore, be pleased to grant your approval (to their plan).

devanagari: तस्मिन् देव क्रतुवरे भवन्तं वै सुरादयः । दिदृक्षवः समेष्यन्ति राजानश्च यशस्विनः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin deva kratuvare bhavantaṃ vai surādayaḥ didṛkṣavaḥ sameṣyanti rājānaśca yaśasvinaḥ

translation: Gods, sages and illustrious kings will certainly attend that grand sacrifice with eagerness to see you.

devanagari: श्रवणात् कीर्तनाद् ध्यानात् पूयन्तेऽन्तेवसायिनः । तव ब्रह्ममयस्येश किमुतेक्षाभिमर्शिनः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: śravaṇāt kīrtanād dhyānāt pūyante tava brahmamayasyeśa kimutekṣābhimarśinaḥ

translation: O Lord! Even (cāṇḍālas and other) outcastes become purified by listening to your stories, muttering your name or glorifying you and by contemplating on you who are Brahman incarnate. What need be said of those who perceive you directly and come in personal contact with you?

devanagari: यस्यामलं दिवि यशः प्रथितं रसायां । भूमौ च ते भुवनमङ्गल दिग्वितानम् । मन्दाकिनीति दिवि भोगवतीति चाधो । गङ्गेति चेह चरणाम्बु पुनाति विश्...
english_devnagari: yasyāmalaṃ divi yaśaḥ prathitaṃ rasāyāṃ bhūmau ca te bhuvanamaṅgala digvitānam mandākinīti divi bhogavatīti cādho gaṅgeti ceha caraṇāmbu punāti viśvam

translation: Your glory which is absolutely pure has enveloped like a canopy all the quarters and has spread out all over the heavenly regions, the earth and the subterranean regions and it renders the world absolutely pure. O Lord! Your very presence is auspicious to the world. The waters that wash your feet are known as the Mandākinī in the heavens, the Bhogavatī in lower regions and as the Gaṅgā in this world and they hallow the universe.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । तत्र तेष्वात्मपक्षेष्व गृह्णत्सु विजिगीषया । वाचः पेशैः स्मयन् भृत्यमुद्धवं प्राह केशवः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca tatra teṣvātmapakṣeṣva gṛhṇatsu vijigīṣayā vācaḥ peśaiḥ smayan bhṛtyamuddhavaṃ prāha keśavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When Yādavas, the clansmen of Kṛṣṇa, being fired with the ambition of vanquishing Jarāsandha were not in a mood to approve of Nārada’s words (but insisted on an expedition against Jarāsandha) instead of going to Ṛājasūya. Kṛṣṇa smilingly spoke in soft words to Uddhava, his devoted and loyal servant.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । त्वं हि नः परमं चक्षुः सुहृन् मंत्रार्थतत्त्ववित् । अथात्र ब्रूह्यनुष्ठेयं श्रद्दध्मः करवाम तत् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca tvaṃ hi naḥ paramaṃ cakṣuḥ suhṛn maṃtrārthatattvavit athātra brūhyanuṣṭheyaṃ śraddadhmaḥ karavāma tat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
“You are our best eye, a well-wisher and knower of the crux of political deliberations. Therefore, advise us what we should do under the circumstances. We believe in you and shall abide by your advice.”

devanagari: इत्युपामंत्रितो भर्त्रा सर्वज्ञेनापि मुग्धवत् । निदेशं शिरसाऽधाय उद्धवः प्रत्यभाषत ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityupāmaṃtrito bhartrā sarvajñenāpi mugdhavat nideśaṃ śirasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ muh class 4 verb]

translation:
When thus called upon to tender advice by his Master, the Omniscient Lord, as if he were an ignorant person, Uddhava respected his order with his head, bowed down and replied (as follows):
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । भगवद्यानविचारे नाम सप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७० ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe bhagavadyānavicāre nāma saptatitamo
